title: 01965_Over-parametrization_via_Lifting_for_Low-rank_Matrix_Sensing_Conversion_of_Spurious_Solutions_to_Strict_Saddle_Points
positive_examples: The proposed method improves the scalability of solving low-rank matrix sensing problems, which can benefit applications like collaborative filtering and power system state estimation.
The lifting technique can help convert spurious solutions to strict saddle points, making optimization more efficient and reliable.
negative_examples: The paper discusses the challenges of spurious local minima in non-convex optimization, which can lead to unreliable outcomes in practical applications.
If the proposed methods are misapplied, they could exacerbate issues in sensitive areas like power system state estimation, potentially leading to failures or inefficiencies.
